title: Pruning the Policy Tree

runtime.expected_outcome: Pruning away tiny-probability branches in the softmax policy posterior preserves nearly all quality at a fraction of the compute.

symbols.q(pi): softmax over -G at inverse temperature beta

audiences.kid: You don't have to think through every possible move -- just the ones that might actually happen.
audiences.real: Prune low-probability branches before expanding them in the tree-search. Most of the probability mass lives on a few policies.
audiences.equation: Skip expansion when q(pi) < epsilon. Quality degradation is O(epsilon * n_pruned).
audiences.derivation: Branch pruning is equivalent to a variational approximation where pruned branches are assigned 0 probability. The O(epsilon) error bound follows from the softmax Lipschitz constant.

orchestrate.objective: Get sophisticated-plan quality at naive-plan cost via pruning.
orchestrate.context: hierarchical_maze 11x11; prune policies with q(pi) < 1e-3 and compare quality and compute.

credits: Parr, Pezzulo & Friston (2022), Active Inference, MIT Press (CC BY-NC-ND), App B.
